The only thing greater than the physical spectacle — the sets, the sheer gallons of water, the extras —  is the dramatic spectacle. From the violinist staying to comfort those amidst the chaos, the captain going down with his ship, or Rose leaping from the lifeboat to be with Jack. This is cinema. Jim Cameron, you are unrivaled.

James Cameron’s epic romantic tragedy beautifully blends old fashioned melodrama with exhilarating thrills and leaves you with an overwhelming sense of bittersweetness when it’s all said n done. DiCaprio n Winslet move you with their touching performances. Visuals and special effects are nothing short of extraordinary with pure breathtaking stuff. A Classic masterpiece that will retain its iconic status for many more decades.

It of course figures that Cameron's crowning achievement as an on-set tyrant and a man hell-bent on drowning at least one above-the-line actor before he dies is also (at least untilThe Way of Water) his most shamelessly earnest and empathetic movie. Instead, the focus is on the innocent people, as they hang on and struggle for dear life.
